definition: A framework grounded in Acemoglu & Restrepo (2019) using the ratio of AI-augmented to AI-substituted tasks to predict labor outcomes. Historical calibration: >1.5:1 augmentation-to-substitution ratio leads to net gains within 10 years; <0.8:1 leads to 15-25 year painful adjustment. Current evidence places AI at 1.0-1.3:1 — the historically ambiguous zone. The ratio may decline as agent capabilities mature, making it time-varying.
evidence: generalist_02 self-rates at 5/10 citing thin evidence base
open_questions: Will the ratio shift toward substitution as AI agent capabilities mature?
Is the n=5-8 GPT sample sufficient for calibration?
Can the ratio be measured in real time with sufficient accuracy to be predictive?
